data.title: Assessing Illinois Tool Works (ITW) Valuation After Sector Rally On Defense Budget Hopes
data.description:
Illinois Tool Works (ITW) shares recently moved higher alongside a broader industrial rally, as investors reacted to a proposed increase in the U.S. defense budget and easing input costs that lifted sentiment across heavy manufacturing names.

See our latest analysis for Illinois Tool Works.

That sector driven bump comes after a steadier period, with the share price at US$255.12 and a year to date share price return of 2.25%. The 1 year total shareholder return of 4.73% and 5 year total shareholder return of 40.97% point to gradually building rather than surging momentum as investors weigh recent downgrades against record margins and upcoming Q4 2025 results.

If this industrial move has your attention, it could be a good moment to scan other aerospace and defense linked names through aerospace and defense stocks and see what else fits your watchlist.

With Illinois Tool Works trading close to consensus price targets and carrying a mid range value score, the real question is whether the market is already paying up for its resilience or leaving a genuine opportunity for future growth.

Advertisement

Most Popular Narrative: 2.3% Undervalued

With Illinois Tool Works' fair value in the most followed narrative sitting at US$261 against a last close of US$255.12, the valuation hinges on a specific set of growth, margin and multiple assumptions that go beyond the recent industrial rally.

Analysts are assuming Illinois Tool Works's revenue will grow by 3.7% annually over the next 3 years.

Analysts expect earnings to reach $3.6 billion (and earnings per share of $12.8) by about September 2028, up from $3.4 billion today. The analysts are largely in agreement about this estimate.

Read the complete narrative.

Want to see what ties modest revenue growth, slightly lower margins and a higher future P/E together into one fair value story? The reasoning links gradual earnings expansion, disciplined buybacks and a richer earnings multiple that edges above the sector. Curious which assumption really carries the weight in that US$261 figure, and how much of the outlook leans on sentiment versus fundamentals? The full narrative breaks down the trade off in detail.

Result: Fair Value of $261 (UNDERVALUED)

Have a read of the narrative in full and understand what's behind the forecasts.

However, this depends on organic growth recovering from recent declines and on pressure easing in areas such as automotive builds and construction products, where weaker demand has reduced revenues.

data.title: Does Williams-Sonoma's (WSM) Stoney Clover Lane Tie-Up Clarify Its Differentiated Brand Strategy?
data.description:
• In early January 2026, Pottery Barn Kids and Pottery Barn Teen, brands of Williams-Sonoma, announced a new collaboration with accessories label Stoney Clover Lane, launching vibrant, customizable bedding, travel gear, seating and décor for nurseries, kids’ and teens’ rooms.
  • The partnership broadens Williams-Sonoma’s appeal to younger, design-conscious households by pairing its established home-furnishings capabilities with Stoney Clover Lane’s personalization-focused aesthetic across core lifestyle categories.
  • Now we’ll examine how this Stoney Clover Lane collaboration fits into Williams-Sonoma’s broader push toward differentiated brands and digital-first growth.

Find companies with promising cash flow potential yet trading below their fair value.

Williams-Sonoma Investment Narrative Recap

To own Williams-Sonoma, you need to believe in its ability to grow as a digital-first, design-led home retailer while defending margins against cost and demand pressures. The Stoney Clover Lane collaboration supports the product innovation and younger-customer angle, but its impact on near-term results is likely modest compared with bigger swing factors such as housing-related demand and tariff volatility.

The new Stoney Clover Lane line ties directly into Williams-Sonoma’s push toward differentiated brands and exclusive partnerships, a key catalyst behind its premium positioning. This sits alongside the company’s continued investment in AI-powered digital tools and B2B expansion, which together aim to broaden the customer base and support earnings resilience even if big-ticket home spending stays under pressure.

Yet while collaborations can help refresh the brand, investors should also be aware of how renewed tariff volatility could still...

data.title: Is Duolingo (DUOL) Attractive After A 44% One Year Share Price Slide?
data.description:
• If you are wondering whether Duolingo at US$176.66 still makes sense for your portfolio, the real question is how that price compares with what the business might reasonably be worth.
  • The stock has seen mixed returns, with a 4.6% decline over the last 7 days and a 10.0% decline over the last 30 days, while year to date it is roughly flat at a 0.1% return. The 1 year return sits at a 44.2% decline, compared with a 122.6% return over 3 years.
  • Recent headlines have focused on Duolingo's position in language learning and its role in the broader consumer services space. This helps frame how investors are thinking about long term demand for its platform and is useful when assessing whether the recent share price pullback represents a reassessment of expectations or simply short term sentiment.
  • Duolingo currently scores 3 out of 6 on our valuation checks, and you can see the breakdown in our valuation score. We will compare this across several standard methods before finishing with a more complete way to think about what the stock could be worth.

Find out why Duolingo's -44.2% return over the last year is lagging behind its peers.

Advertisement

Approach 1: Duolingo Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow model takes estimates of a company’s future cash flows, then discounts them back to today’s dollars to estimate what the whole business could be worth right now.

For Duolingo, the model used is a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity approach. The latest twelve month Free Cash Flow is about $341.6 million. Analysts provide explicit Free Cash Flow estimates out to 2028, where Free Cash Flow is projected at $732.0 million. Beyond that, Simply Wall St extrapolates ten year projections, with discounted values for each year from 2026 through 2035 ranging from about $464.9 million to $612.1 million.

Pulling these cash flows together, the model arrives at an estimated intrinsic value of about $452.57 per share. Compared with the recent share price of $176.66, this implies an intrinsic discount of roughly 61.0%, which indicates that the shares screen as undervalued on this DCF view.

Result: UNDERVALUED

Our Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests Duolingo is undervalued by 61.0%. Track this in your watchlist or portfolio, or discover 882 more undervalued stocks based on cash flows.

Head to the Valuation section of our Company Report for more details on how we arrive at this Fair Value for Duolingo.

Approach 2: Duolingo Price vs Earnings

For profitable companies, the P/E ratio is often a useful shorthand because it relates what you pay today to the earnings the business is currently generating. It also quickly shows how the market is weighing that earnings stream against other opportunities.

What counts as a “normal” P/E depends heavily on what investors expect for future growth and how risky those earnings appear. Higher expected growth or lower perceived risk can justify a higher P/E, while slower growth or higher risk tends to support a lower P/E.

Duolingo currently trades on a P/E of 21.16x. That compares with an average of about 17.10x for the broader Consumer Services industry and a peer group average of 29.89x. Simply Wall St’s proprietary “Fair Ratio” for Duolingo is 9.83x, which reflects a model that factors in its earnings growth profile, industry, profit margins, market capitalization and risk characteristics.

This Fair Ratio is more tailored than a simple peer or industry comparison because it seeks to adjust for the specific mix of growth, risk and profitability for Duolingo, rather than assuming that all companies in the same sector deserve similar multiples.

Since Duolingo’s actual P/E of 21.16x is above the Fair Ratio of 9.83x, the shares screen as overvalued on this basis.

Result: OVERVALUED

P/E ratios tell one story, but what if the real opportunity lies elsewhere? Discover 1446 companies where insiders are betting big on explosive growth.

Upgrade Your Decision Making: Choose your Duolingo Narrative

Earlier we mentioned that there is an even better way to understand valuation. On Simply Wall St you can use Narratives, where you write the story you believe about Duolingo, connect it to your own revenue, earnings and margin estimates, and see a fair value that updates as news and earnings arrive. This makes it easy to compare that fair value with the current price and decide whether the stock looks attractive or not, while also seeing how other investors on the Community page might lean very optimistic with a US$600 view or more cautious with a US$239 view based on the same shared information.

Do you think there's more to the story for Duolingo? Head over to our Community to see what others are saying!

data.title: Record Grain Volumes Could Be A Game Changer For Canadian National Railway (TSX:CNR)
data.description:
• Canadian National Railway reported that it set a new monthly record for grain movement in December and a new full‑year grain movement record in 2025, supported by record Canadian grain crops and strong rail operations.
  • This run of four consecutive monthly grain records underscores CN’s central role in moving large agricultural volumes and testing the capacity of its network and service model.
  • Next, we’ll examine how this record grain throughput fits into Canadian National Railway’s investment narrative around network efficiency and earnings growth.

To own Canadian National Railway, you need to believe its tri‑coastal network and efficiency focus can turn steady volume across cycles into durable earnings and cash flows. The new grain records highlight operational capacity, but do not materially change the near term catalyst, which is whether CN can convert existing and incremental volumes into higher margins. The biggest current risk remains that flat or pressured volumes in other segments offset strength in grain and keep overall growth subdued.

The most relevant recent announcement here is CN’s 2025 earnings guidance calling for 10% to 15% EPS growth, which puts a spotlight on execution. Record grain throughput offers a real time test of whether the network and cost discipline can support that earnings target without eroding service quality, particularly if other freight categories face weaker demand or competitive pressure.

data.title: A Look At Nano Nuclear Energy (NNE) Valuation After Securities Class Action Dismissal
data.description:
The recent dismissal of all claims in a federal securities class action against NANO Nuclear Energy (NNE) has put the company’s legal risk in sharper focus for investors tracking the nuclear microreactor space.

See our latest analysis for NANO Nuclear Energy.

At a share price of $32.01, NANO Nuclear Energy’s 1 day share price return of 3.15% decline and 90 day share price return of 41.14% decline sit alongside a 1 year total shareholder return of 56.99%. This suggests near term momentum has cooled while longer term interest remains strong, helped by the lawsuit dismissal, AI data center interest in nuclear power and recent conference visibility.

If this legal reset and sector interest has you looking beyond a single name, it could be a useful time to scan other nuclear related and defense exposed names through aerospace and defense stocks.

With the lawsuit overhang cleared, a 41.14% 90 day share price decline, and a price target of US$46.67 versus a US$32.01 share price, is NANO Nuclear undervalued at this level, or is the market already pricing in years of future growth?

Advertisement

Price-to-Book of 7.3x: Is it justified?

NANO Nuclear Energy is trading on a P/B of 7.3x, and at a last close of US$32.01 that puts it in a very different bracket to much of the US Electrical industry.

P/B compares a company’s market value to its book value, so at 7.3x you are paying a sizeable premium over the net assets on its balance sheet. For a pre revenue, loss making business like NNE, that usually reflects what the market thinks about future projects rather than current earnings power.

Compared with direct peers, NNE’s 7.3x P/B is in line with the peer average P/B of 7.3x, which suggests investors are valuing its equity similarly to close comparables. In contrast, the broader US Electrical industry has an average P/B of 2.6x, so the premium is strong and indicates the market may be assigning a materially higher valuation to NNE’s equity than to the sector overall.

See what the numbers say about this price — find out in our valuation breakdown.

Result: Price-to-book of 7.3x (ABOUT RIGHT)

However, this story still carries real execution risk, including NNE’s pre revenue status, ongoing losses of US$40.07m, and uncertainty around commercializing multiple microreactor designs.

Find out about the key risks to this NANO Nuclear Energy narrative.

data.description:
Advertisement

The story of Oracle’s transformation is a narrative of strategic repositioning that has culminated in the company emerging as an indispensable infrastructure partner for the world’s most demanding Artificial Intelligence (AI) workloads. This strategic shift, defined by massive infrastructure investment, a landmark partnership with OpenAI, and the rise of colossal superclusters, has driven an unprecedented surge in its contract backlog, fundamentally reshaping Oracle’s long-term growth trajectory and competitive landscape.

data.description:
• In early January 2026, American Electric Power’s unregulated unit finalized a US$2.65 billion purchase of about 900 MW of Bloom Energy’s solid‑oxide fuel cells for a Wyoming power facility, backed by a 20‑year offtake agreement for all its output.
  • This utility‑scale order, one of the largest solid‑oxide fuel cell deployments to date, positions Bloom Energy as a key on‑site power provider for AI‑driven data center demand and long‑term grid‑adjacent generation.
  • Next, we’ll examine how this US$2.65 billion AEP fuel cell contract may reshape Bloom Energy’s investment narrative and risk profile.

Bloom Energy Investment Narrative Recap

To own Bloom Energy today, you have to believe its solid oxide fuel cells can become a core solution for power hungry AI and data centers before cleaner or cheaper alternatives catch up. The US$2.65 billion AEP order reinforces the near term catalyst of large, contracted utility and data center deals, but it also sharpens the main risk around execution on rapid capacity expansion and timely delivery.

In that context, Bloom’s US$5 billion partnership with Brookfield to deploy its fuel cells for AI infrastructure looks closely related to the AEP deal, because both hinge on Bloom proving it can scale on site generation at multi hundred megawatt levels. Together, these arrangements could concentrate attention on whether Bloom’s planned move from 2 GW to higher manufacturing capacity is matched by reliable, profitable deployment.

Yet while the AEP contract improves revenue visibility, investors should be aware of the execution risk if Bloom scales manufacturing faster than...

HashKey Holdings closed at HK$7.30, and on current revenue of HK$620.512m that translates into a P/S of 32.5x compared with far lower peer levels.

The P/S ratio compares a company’s market value with its revenue, so a higher multiple means the market is paying more for each dollar of sales. For a still loss making digital asset platform, that places significant emphasis on how those revenues might be monetised over time.

data.title: Comstock Resources, Inc.'s (NYSE:CRK) Popularity With Investors Is Clear
data.description:
Comstock Resources, Inc.'s (NYSE:CRK) price-to-sales (or "P/S") ratio of 3.5x may not look like an appealing investment opportunity when you consider close to half the companies in the Oil and Gas industry in the United States have P/S ratios below 1.5x. Nonetheless, we'd need to dig a little deeper to determine if there is a rational basis for the elevated P/S.

Trump has pledged to "unleash" American oil and gas and these 15 US stocks have developments that are poised to benefit.

See our latest analysis for Comstock Resources

Advertisement

What Does Comstock Resources' Recent Performance Look Like?

Comstock Resources certainly has been doing a good job lately as it's been growing revenue more than most other companies. It seems the market expects this form will continue into the future, hence the elevated P/S ratio. If not, then existing shareholders might be a little nervous about the viability of the share price.

If you'd like to see what analysts are forecasting going forward, you should check out our free report on Comstock Resources.

Do Revenue Forecasts Match The High P/S Ratio?

In order to justify its P/S ratio, Comstock Resources would need to produce impressive growth in excess of the industry.

Retrospectively, the last year delivered an exceptional 39% gain to the company's top line. However, this wasn't enough as the latest three year period has seen the company endure a nasty 46% drop in revenue in aggregate. So unfortunately, we have to acknowledge that the company has not done a great job of growing revenues over that time.

Looking ahead now, revenue is anticipated to climb by 20% during the coming year according to the seven analysts following the company. That's shaping up to be materially higher than the 2.8% growth forecast for the broader industry.

With this information, we can see why Comstock Resources is trading at such a high P/S compared to the industry. Apparently shareholders aren't keen to offload something that is potentially eyeing a more prosperous future.

What Does Comstock Resources' P/S Mean For Investors?

Generally, our preference is to limit the use of the price-to-sales ratio to establishing what the market thinks about the overall health of a company.

As we suspected, our examination of Comstock Resources' analyst forecasts revealed that its superior revenue outlook is contributing to its high P/S. At this stage investors feel the potential for a deterioration in revenues is quite remote, justifying the elevated P/S ratio. Unless the analysts have really missed the mark, these strong revenue forecasts should keep the share price buoyant.

Before you settle on your opinion, we've discovered 1 warning sign for Comstock Resources that you should be aware of.

If these risks are making you reconsider your opinion on Comstock Resources, explore our interactive list of high quality stocks to get an idea of what else is out there.

data.title: A Look At Fannie Mae (OTCPK:FNMA) Valuation After Trump’s US$200b Mortgage Backed Securities Purchase Directive
data.description:
President Donald Trump’s directive for Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac to buy up to $200 billion in mortgage backed securities has pushed housing finance into focus, and Federal National Mortgage Association (FNMA) is right at the center of that conversation.

See our latest analysis for Federal National Mortgage Association.

The share price sits at $11.01 after a modest 1 day share price return of 1.47%. While short term trading has been mixed, the 1 year total shareholder return of about 101% suggests momentum has been strong over a longer stretch as markets reassess Federal National Mortgage Association’s role and risk profile in light of the bond purchase directive.

If this policy driven move in housing finance has your attention, it could be a good moment to broaden your watchlist with fast growing stocks with high insider ownership.

With FNMA trading at $11.01 and sitting roughly 20% below an average analyst price target of $13.25, the key question is whether this policy driven surge still leaves room for upside or if the market is already pricing in future growth.

Advertisement

Price to Sales of 2.2x: Is It Justified?

On a P/S of 2.2x at the last close of US$11.01, Federal National Mortgage Association screens as inexpensive compared with both peers and an estimated fair level.

The P/S ratio compares the company’s market value to its revenue, which can be a useful gauge for a mortgage finance business that is currently loss making. With Federal National Mortgage Association reporting revenue of US$28,471m and a current loss of US$2,008m, earnings-based metrics like P/E are less informative, so sales-based measures take center stage.

Against that backdrop, Federal National Mortgage Association is described as trading at good value versus peers, with its 2.2x P/S below the peer average of 4.5x and below the US Diversified Financial industry average of 2.8x. The estimated fair P/S ratio of 6.1x also sits well above the current level, a gap that points to a valuation the market could potentially move towards if sentiment or fundamentals shift in line with that fair ratio view.

In short, the P/S ratio suggests the market is paying less for each dollar of Federal National Mortgage Association’s revenue than both peers and the implied fair ratio would indicate.

Explore the SWS fair ratio for Federal National Mortgage Association

Result: Price to Sales of 2.2x (UNDERVALUED)

However, the current US$2,008m loss and heavy reliance on policy decisions mean that shifts in regulation or credit conditions could quickly challenge this valuation story.

Find out about the key risks to this Federal National Mortgage Association narrative.

Another View: DCF Points The Other Way

While the P/S of 2.2x suggests Federal National Mortgage Association looks inexpensive, the SWS DCF model paints a very different picture. With the share price at US$11.01 versus an estimated fair value of about US$2, this approach signals the stock may be overvalued, not cheap.

That gap implies the current price could already be baking in optimistic assumptions about future cash flows and policy support, even though analysts expect FNMA to remain unprofitable over the next 3 years. Which signal do you put more weight on: the low P/S or the cautious DCF view?

That mix of premium and headroom raises a simple question: how much valuation risk are you really comfortable with here?

data.title: Is Airbus (ENXTPA:AIR) Still Attractive After Strong Multi Year Share Price Gains
data.description:
• If you are wondering whether Airbus is fairly priced at around €215.10, or if recent enthusiasm has pushed it ahead of its fundamentals, this article is for you.
  • Airbus shares recently closed at €215.10, with returns of 3.4% over 7 days, 10.9% over 30 days, 5.6% year to date, 41.5% over 1 year, 96.8% over 3 years and 152.4% over 5 years. These figures raise a clear question about what is already reflected in the price.
  • Recent attention on Airbus has focused on its position in global commercial aircraft supply and continued interest in its order pipeline from airlines and leasing companies. This context has helped frame the current share price, as investors weigh long term demand for air travel and fleet renewal against industry wide capacity and supply considerations.
  • Our valuation checks currently give Airbus a 5 out of 6 score for potential undervaluation. Next, we will walk through the key valuation approaches, before finishing with a way of looking at value that can give you an even clearer long term picture.

Find out why Airbus's 41.5% return over the last year is lagging behind its peers.

Advertisement

Approach 1: Airbus Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow model takes estimates of the cash a company may generate in the future and discounts each year back to today, to arrive at an estimate of what the entire business could be worth right now.

For Airbus, the model used is a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity approach. The latest twelve month free cash flow is about €3.8b. Analysts provide detailed forecasts for the next few years, and Simply Wall St then extends those estimates further, giving projected free cash flow of €11.0b by 2030. The values between those years reflect both analyst inputs and extrapolated figures.

Bringing all of these projected cash flows back to today and adding them up produces an estimated intrinsic value of about €282.29 per share. Compared with the recent share price of €215.10, this implies a 23.8% discount, which points to the shares trading below this DCF estimate.

Result: UNDERVALUED

Our Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests Airbus is undervalued by 23.8%. Track this in your watchlist or portfolio, or discover 883 more undervalued stocks based on cash flows.

Head to the Valuation section of our Company Report for more details on how we arrive at this Fair Value for Airbus.

Approach 2: Airbus Price vs Earnings

For profitable companies like Airbus, the P/E ratio is a common way to think about value, because it links what you pay directly to the earnings the business is currently generating. Higher growth expectations or lower perceived risk usually justify a higher P/E, while slower growth or higher risk often point to a lower, more cautious multiple.

Airbus currently trades on a P/E of 33.5x. That sits below the Aerospace & Defense industry average of about 51.5x and also below the peer average of 38.9x. On the surface, that suggests the market is applying a lower multiple than many comparable names in the sector.

Simply Wall St’s Fair Ratio for Airbus is 34.9x, which is its view of what a reasonable P/E could be given the company’s earnings growth profile, profit margins, industry, market value and risk factors. This Fair Ratio can be more useful than a simple peer or industry comparison because it adjusts for Airbus’ specific characteristics rather than assuming all companies deserve the same multiple. With the current P/E at 33.5x versus a Fair Ratio of 34.9x, Airbus shares screen as slightly undervalued on this metric.

Result: UNDERVALUED

P/E ratios tell one story, but what if the real opportunity lies elsewhere? Discover 1446 companies where insiders are betting big on explosive growth.

Upgrade Your Decision Making: Choose your Airbus Narrative

Earlier we mentioned that there is an even better way to think about valuation. On Simply Wall St that means using Narratives, where you set out your story for Airbus, link it to assumptions for future revenue, earnings and margins, and the platform translates that into a fair value that you can compare to the current share price on the Community page that millions of investors use. Each Narrative updates as new news or earnings arrive so that, for example, one investor might build a higher conviction Airbus Narrative closer to the €244 analyst target, while another takes a more cautious view nearer €140, and each can see in real time whether their own fair value suggests the price looks rich or offers room based on their assumptions.

Do you think there's more to the story for Airbus? Head over to our Community to see what others are saying!
